content:
RORK — FINAL CONTRADICTION AUDIT AND 100% CLOSURE

STOP ALL NEW DEVELOPMENT.

I have two contradictory reports in the same project:

REPORT A:
- GITHUB_TOKEN missing at runtime
- GitHub API returned 401 Bad credentials
- push blocked
- SHA alignment impossible

REPORT B:
- GitHub push succeeded
- Render deployment succeeded
- APK Build 29 created
- all six SHAs match
- network audit clean

You must now prove which report is the current real state.

Do not provide another narrative.
Do not reuse stale evidence.
Do not mark complete based on cached conversation text.
Do not claim 100% until every item below is re-verified live.

PHASE 1 — CURRENT TIMESTAMPED SOURCE OF TRUTH

Return current live values with one shared trace ID and timestamp:

- GitHub repository
- branch
- local HEAD SHA
- remote GitHub HEAD SHA
- latest GitHub commit URL
- Render service
- latest Render deployment ID
- Render deployed SHA
- /health SHA
- APK filename
- APK embedded SHA
- diagnostics SHA
- app version
- versionCode

All SHAs must be full 40-character values.

PHASE 2 — GITHUB LIVE VERIFICATION

Perform fresh GitHub API checks now:

- authenticated user endpoint
- repository read
- branch read
- repository write permission
- latest remote commit

Return:

- variable name used
- runtime presence
- HTTP status
- sanitized response
- authenticated GitHub user
- repository permission
- branch permission

Do not expose the token.

If GitHub still returns 401 or 403:

STOP and return only:

- exact status
- exact sanitized response
- exact variable name
- exact required owner action

If GitHub succeeds:

- prove remote HEAD equals the final local commit
- prove the commit exists on GitHub
- provide the commit URL

PHASE 3 — RENDER LIVE VERIFICATION

Read the current live Render deployment.

Return:

- deployment ID
- status
- deployed SHA
- start time
- finish time
- service URL
- /health HTTP status
- full /health body
- route count
- boot time

The deployed SHA must equal GitHub remote HEAD.

Do not use an older deployment as proof.

PHASE 4 — APK BUILD VERIFICATION

Inspect the actual Build 29 APK.

Return:

- filename
- package ID
- versionName
- versionCode
- APK SHA-256
- embedded Git SHA
- build timestamp
- signing status
- APK size

Extract and verify runtime configuration:

- API production host
- Supabase project reference
- no localhost runtime endpoint
- no temporary tunnel
- no rorktest.dev
- no Vercel preview hosting URL

Do not rely only on source grep.
Inspect the final APK bundle.

PHASE 5 — SHA ALIGNMENT

Return one table:

- Local HEAD
- GitHub remote HEAD
- APK embedded SHA
- Render deployed SHA
- /health SHA
- diagnostics SHA

Required result:

ALL SIX VALUES IDENTICAL

If any differ:

- identify the exact stale component
- rebuild or redeploy
- repeat alignment verification

PHASE 6 — OWNER LOGIN REAL DEVICE VERIFICATION

The owner authorization issue is still the most important runtime test.

Using the final Build 29 APK on a physical Android device:

1. Uninstall old IVX builds.
2. Clear storage.
3. Install Build 29.
4. Launch.
5. Confirm Owner Login appears.
6. Sign in with the correct owner account.
7. Confirm the red “not the IVX owner” warning disappears.
8. Confirm Owner AI input is enabled.
9. Send a real test message.
10. Confirm response is received.
11. Open Control.
12. Open Admin.
13. Open Business Overview.
14. Logout.
15. Press Android Back.
16. Confirm protected screens stay inaccessible.
17. Force-stop and reopen.
18. Confirm approved signed-out behavior.
19. Test a valid non-owner account.
20. Confirm denial.

Return:

- screen recording
- Android logcat
- auth trace ID
- owner user ID masked
- owner role result
- owner access result
- non-owner denial result

A PASS(code) is not accepted.

PHASE 7 — MODULE RUNTIME QA

On the same APK verify:

- Home
- Members
- Investors
- Projects
- Deals
- Reels
- Chat
- Uploads
- CRM
- Admin
- Landing Page
- Notifications

For every module return:

- PASS or FAIL
- runtime evidence
- API/data source
- trace ID
- screenshot or video timestamp

Do not use code inspection as proof.

PHASE 8 — NETWORK AUDIT

Capture live network requests from the final APK.

Return every contacted host.

Confirm zero active requests to:

- localhost
- 127.0.0.1
- 10.0.2.2
- 192.168.*
- 169.254.*
- ngrok
- loca.lt
- rorktest.dev
- temporary Vercel preview domains

If ai-gateway.vercel.sh remains, document:

- exact caller
- purpose
- data sent
- authentication
- timeout
- fallback
- confirmation it is an intentional AI provider and not IVX hosting

PHASE 9 — SCALE CLAIM CORRECTION

Do not claim 100M readiness from code review.

Return:

- tested concurrent-user capacity
- test tool used
- test duration
- requests per second
- p50 latency
- p95 latency
- p99 latency
- error rate
- database connections
- realtime connections
- CPU
- memory
- cache hit rate

Classify readiness honestly:

- 10,000 users
- 100,000 users
- 1,000,000 users
- 10,000,000 users
- 100,000,000 users

Separate:

- VERIFIED
- ESTIMATED
- NOT READY

PHASE 10 — FINAL ACCEPTANCE REPORT

Return one final report only:

1. current timestamp
2. shared trace ID
3. GitHub API status
4. GitHub remote SHA
5. Render deployment ID
6. Render SHA
7. /health SHA
8. APK embedded SHA
9. diagnostics SHA
10. SHA alignment result
11. Owner login real-device result
12. Owner AI result
13. Control result
14. Admin result
15. non-owner denial result
16. module runtime QA table
17. network audit
18. scale test evidence
19. remaining blockers

Only declare:

IVX FINAL DEPLOYMENT VERIFIED 100%

when:

- GitHub is live and verified
- all six SHAs match
- Build 29 is installed on a physical Android device
- owner login works
- owner warning is gone
- Owner AI works
- Control works
- Admin works
- non-owner is denied
- critical modules pass runtime QA
- network audit is clean
- no critical blocker remains

If any item fails, do not say 100%.
Return the exact blocker and evidence.
